Summary: In ACP mice, P3F (Pax3-FOXO1) expression reprogrammed aP2-Cre–labeled cells to acquire muscle stem cell properties capable of muscle regeneration. P3F expression produced tumors in all 24 ACP mice but did not generate fusion-positive rhabdomyosarcoma. Pax3-FOXO1 was present in three ACP tumors. None of the three Pax3-Foxo1-positive ACP tumors expressed the myogenic regulatory transcription factors Myod1 or Myog that are diagnostic for rhabdomyosarcoma. This fusion joins the PAX3 DNA-binding domain with the transactivation domain of FOXO1. The presence of PAX3-FOXO1 foretells a worse prognosis. PAX3-FOXO1 is one of the fusion oncoproteins that define fusion-positive RMS. Classifying tumors based on fusion status (fusion-positive, which includes PAX3-FOXO1, versus fusion-negative) more accurately categorizes RMS patients. High-risk fusion-positive RMS can present without an identifiable primary tumor and may show widespread disseminated disease or solely bone marrow involvement.
